I am writing to formally log a severe, life-threatening suspected adverse drug reaction regarding your oral small-molecule agent, CholoClear-X (50mg formulation).

She is currently stabilized but remains in critical condition. No concomitant medications were introduced prior to the event, and she has no history of clinical trial enrollment.

Please log this case immediately. Address any inquiries or requests for follow-up to my desk.

Dr. Samuel Brennan, MD, FACC

labeling_notes: WHAT THE HARNESS SCORES: a halted case is scored ONLY on routing_target == 'Unreadable Queue'. No classification or extraction is produced (the pipeline returns at check_readability), so there is no class/record/gate ground truth to compare — by design.

REAL-WORLD PARADOX (the interview point): the text contains critical safety keywords ('hepatic failure', 'ICU', '15-day clock'), but the OCR quality (0.60) is below the 0.75 trust floor. The system does NOT try to parse a garbled safety signal — a mis-read AE is more dangerous than an escalated one.
GATE 1 PRE-CLASSIFY HALT: OCR < 0.75 triggers a hard stop BEFORE the LLM is called (no model cost spent on junk text). Routed straight to the Unreadable Queue for human transcription.

AE-RECALL NUANCE: this unreadable AE is NOT counted against the agent's AE-recall floor — the AI did not 'miss' it, it correctly escalated it to a human. The agent's job on unreadable docs is to ESCALATE, not extract.

DAY 0 INTEGRITY: the agent does not compute a due date here (it can't read the doc). But Day 0 is preserved: received_date (2026-06-08) is stamped at INGESTION, in the envelope — so when a human transcribes the fax, the regulatory clock back-dates to intake, not to the (later) transcription time.

labeling_notes: GATE 1 (stable) + CONFIDENCE FLUTTER (calibration finding): Gate 1 always fails on the OCR band (0.82) -> MIS, so the OUTCOME is stable. The classifier confidence on this same clean On-Label question FLUTTERS across runs (observed 0.82 / 0.88 / 0.91), so Gate 2 sometimes co-fires and sometimes doesn't — it sits right on the 0.90 line. Takeaway: a single confidence reading near the threshold is noisy; the 0.90 gate flips borderline cases run-to-run (reinforces the 0.95->0.90 calibration story; the robust Gate-2 failure lives in case_015 at 0.62). Gate 4/5/6/8 all pass; Gate 1 alone drives MIS.

WHY OCR 0.82 ON CLEAN TEXT: OCR confidence is the engine's CERTAINTY about its own output (driven by scan skew, faintness, resolution), which is a separate signal from human legibility. The 0.75-0.90 tier exists precisely for this: don't fully trust the scan even when it looks fine -> force a human to verify. (Contrast case_011, where the text was genuinely garbled at 0.60 and halted pre-classify.)

DESIGN FINDING: an earlier draft used a STORAGE-excursion question with visible scan noise; it scored only ~0.82 classifier confidence (the noise depresses the model's confidence, and 'storage' isn't named in our On-Label taxonomy description), tripping Gate 2 as well. We separated the concerns: keep #12 clean to isolate Gate 1, and reuse that storage/noise scenario for the Gate 2 case (#13).
SRD RETRIEVAL: the dosage question maps to a pre-approved SRD (SRD_CHOLO_DOSAGE_ADULT_v2, the 50mg/day ceiling) — the AI SUGGESTS it for the reviewer, never authors or sends.
DAY 0: received_date 2026-06-08 is stamped, but no regulatory_due_date is computed — the 15/90-day clock applies only to AE cases.

Dear Medical Affairs,

We have a storage-stability question regarding your commercial lipid-lowering tablet, CholoClear-X (50mg oral).

A sealed bottle of CholoClear-X tablets was inadvertently left in a clinic delivery vehicle over a long weekend, where the internal temperature is estimated to have reached approximately 40 degrees C for around 30 hours, exceeding the labeled storage range.

The How Supplied / Storage and Handling section of the package insert notes a recommended storage temperature but does not specify how to handle a transient excursion of this magnitude. Could you confirm whether this heat excursion requires us to quarantine and discard the bottle, or whether stability is still acceptable for dispensing?

Priya Nair, PharmD
Cleveland Clinic
Email: p.nair@clevelandclinic.org | Phone: 216-555-0133
labeling_notes: OUT-OF-TAXONOMY: storage/handling is not one of our 10 classes, so the per-class scorer spreads its scores (no class scores high). The top is Formulation at ~0.62 — low confidence, which is the CORRECT signal (the model genuinely has no clean home for this).

CRASH-SAFETY (unbuilt class -> MIS): Formulation has no automated extractor yet, so rather than crash (NotImplementedError) the pipeline fails safe to MIS with the reason logged ('class has no automated extractor yet -> routed to human review'). A halted/early-return path, so no per-gate trace is produced — scored only on routing_target.

WHY THIS IS A GOOD CASE: it proves two safeguards at once — (1) per-class scoring surfaces genuine taxonomy gaps as low confidence, and (2) the system degrades gracefully (human review) instead of forcing a wrong class or crashing. Roadmap: add a Storage/Handling class, or a Formulation extractor.

HISTORY: pre-Option-A this case self-reported as On-Label at 0.72 and was our Gate-2 isolation. Per-class scoring revealed the truer picture (no clean fit). Gate-2 firing is still visible here via the low 0.62 confidence; case_015 also exercises low confidence.

labeling_notes: SHOWSTOPPER: The email's surface ask is an FAQ (brochure/coupon link). A naive classifier routes this to Public Health/FAQ Deflection. The hidden adverse event (chest burning, irregular racing heartbeat, dizziness) must dominate -> classify as Adverse Event Report, route to PV. Proves AE detection beats surface classification.

The PATIENT is the same person but must be stored as initials only ('M.V.'), NOT the full name, per HIPAA/GDPR/ICH. contact_email is null (info-reply@cardiomedglobal.com is the COMPANY inbound address, not Marcus's email); only his mobile + home address were given.

SERIOUSNESS — labeled Serious (calibrated decision; originally a coin-flip). The symptoms (chest burning + irregular racing heartbeat + dizziness) read as a possible cardiac arrhythmia. Under ICH E2A this is defensible as life-threatening (criterion b) or an Important Medical Event. We chose Serious over Non-Serious because: (1) the ICH-E2A-grounded prompt lands here STABLY (5/5 runs) once the vague 'medically important' wording was tightened to the standard IME bar; (2) over-calling seriousness errs in the SAFE direction for PV triage, while under-calling a cardiac signal is the dangerous error; (3) the original label was explicitly flagged 'defensible either way'. record.off_label_indication: Use of CholoClear-X at 100mg (above approved 50mg maximum) in a pediatric patient aged 9 years, outside the approved adult-only indication
